chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Crl. Bail Application No. 76 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES For hearing of bail application. 10-03-2023 Ms. Nasima Mangrio, Advocate for applicant. Ms. Abida Parveen Channer, Spl. Prosecutor, ANF. ============= Omar Sial, J: Rehmatullah has sought post arrest bail in crime number 06 of 2022 registered under sections 6 and 9(c) of the CNS Act, 1997 at the Gulshan-e-Iqbal police station. Earlier his application seeking bail was dismissed by the learned Special Court-1 (CNS) Karachi on 28-05-2022. 2. The case is that an ANF police party led by Inspector Muzammil Ahmed during a snap checking duty to detect narcotics stopped a vehicle with a Government number plate and which was coming from Balochistan towards Karachi. 2 persons were present in the vehicle who were subsequently identified as Kala Khan and Rehmat Ali. The latter being the applicant herein. 270 kilograms of charas was recovered from the vehicle. 3. It is pertinent to point out that the same applicant had earlier file a bail application in this court (being Crl. Bail App No. 1393 of 2022) which was dismissed on 12.09.2022 with the directions that the trial court completes the trial preferably within 3 months. It was not a dismissal simpliciter as the directions had come after having heard the counsel at length. The learned counsel was therefore asked as to what new ground did she have in support of her bail application as non-compliance of a time frame given by the court was hardly a ground for admitting the applicant to bail. Learned counsel while being unable to highlight any fresh ground showed her desire to once again re-agitate the grounds she had agitated when the earlier bail application was dismissed.

The learned counsel was therefore asked as to what new ground did she have in support of her bail application as non-compliance of a time frame given by the court was hardly a ground for admitting the applicant to bail. Learned counsel while being unable to highlight any fresh ground showed her desire to once again re-agitate the grounds she had agitated when the earlier bail application was dismissed. This, the law does not permit. 4. No fresh ground having been argued, the bail application stands dismissed. JUDGE
